journal_entry.description: Conversion of preferred stock to common stock
journal_entry.rationale: This is an equity restructuring transaction where preferred shares are converted to common shares. Under IFRS, this is a direct equity-to-equity transfer. The carrying amount of the preferred stock is removed (debit Preferred Stock to decrease equity) and the same amount is added to common stock (credit Common Stock to increase equity). This is a non-taxable transaction as it involves no cash flow or revenue/expense recognition—only a reclassification within the equity section of the balance sheet.

output.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock is an equity reclassification. On the credit side, we increase Common Stock (equity increase). The debit side would decrease Preferred Stock (equity decrease). Since we only classify the credit side, we have one line: Common Stock = equity increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock is an equity reclassification. On the credit side, we increase Common Stock (equity increase). The debit side would decrease Preferred Stock (equity decrease). Since we only classify the credit side, we have one line: Common Stock = equity increase.

output.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock involves removing (decreasing) the Preferred Stock equity account. Under IFRS, both preferred and common stock are equity accounts. Debiting equity decreases it, so this is 1 equity decrease (slot e). The corresponding credit would increase Common Stock equity, but we only classify the debit side.

debit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock involves removing (decreasing) the Preferred Stock equity account. Under IFRS, both preferred and common stock are equity accounts. Debiting equity decreases it, so this is 1 equity decrease (slot e). The corresponding credit would increase Common Stock equity, but we only classify the debit side.
